blockOutgoing(uint16,uint64,bool).details: Used during adapter migrations to ensure no messages are in-flight while the adapter         configuration is being updated. See `IHub.setAdapters` for the full procedure.

params.canSend: If can send messages or not
params.centrifugeId: Centrifuge ID associated to this block

params.what: The name of the variable to be updated

returns._0: Whether the gateway is currently batching

lockCallback().details: calling this at the very beginning inside the multicall means:         - The callback is called from the gateway under `withBatch`.         - The callback is called from the same contract, because withBatch uses `msg.sender` as target for the callback         - The callback that uses this can only be called once inside withBatch scope. No reentrancy.

recoverTokens(address,address,uint256).details: This method is called only by authorized entities

params.amount: Amount to send to the receiver.
params.token: It could be 0xeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee         to recover locked native ETH or token compatible with ERC20.

recoverTokens(address,uint256,address,ui.details: This method is called only by authorized entities

params.amount: Amount to send to the receiver.
params.token: It could be 0xeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee         to recover locked native ETH or token compatible with ERC20 or ERC6909.
params.tokenId: The token id, i.e. non-zero if the underlying token is ERC6909 and else zero.

params.unpaidMode: Tells if storing the message as unpaid if not enough funds

withBatch(bytes,uint256,address).details: Should be used like:         ```         contract Integration {             IGateway gateway;             function doSomething(PoolId poolId) external {                 gateway.withBatch(abi.encodeWithSelector(Integration.callback.selector, poolId));             }             function callback(PoolId poolId) external {                 // Avoid reentrancy to the callback and ensure it's called from withBatch in the same contract:                 gateway.lockCallback();                 // Call several hub, balance sheet, or spoke methods that trigger cross-chain transactions             }         }         ```         NOTE: inside callback, `msgSender` should be used instead of msg.sender

params.callbackData: encoding data for the callback method
params.callbackValue: msg.value to send to the callback

blockOutgoing(uint16,uint64,bool).notice: Block or unblock outgoing messages for a pool on a specific chain.

file(bytes32,address).notice: Used to update an address (state variable) on very rare occasions

isBatching().notice: Returns the current gateway batching level

lockCallback().notice: Ensures the callback is called by withBatch in the same contract.

recoverTokens(address,address,uint256).notice: Used to recover any ERC-20 token.

recoverTokens(address,uint256,address,ui.notice: Used to recover any ERC-20 or ERC-6909 token.

updateManager(uint64,address,bool).notice: Configures a manager address for a pool

withBatch(bytes,address).notice: Same as withBatch(..), but without sending any msg.value to the callback

withBatch(bytes,uint256,address).notice: Automatic batching of cross-chain transactions through a callback.         Any cross-chain transactions triggered in this callback will automatically be batched.
